budget_wines.name: Vietti Tre Vigne Barbera d'Asti DOCG
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Vietti cellar door in Castiglione Falletto; widely distributed across Italian wine shops and US importers.
budget_wines.description: Barbera from three Asti parcels, aged about a year between steel, barrique and large oak. Ruby-red, sour-cherry fruit, soft tannin and bright acidity make it the benchmark everyday Piedmont red.
budget_wines.tip: Serve it lightly chilled with a tomato-sauce pasta; the acidity cuts the sauce better than any Nebbiolo would.

budget_wines.name: G.D. Vajra Dolcetto d'Alba DOC
budget_wines.where_to_buy: G.D. Vajra cellar at Vergne, Barolo; distributed widely in Italy, the UK and the US through Liberty Wines and others.
budget_wines.description: Certified-organic Dolcetto from the highest hamlet of Barolo. Inky purple, plummy and faintly almond-bitter on the finish, it is the textbook fruity, low-acid Piedmont weeknight red.
budget_wines.tip: Drink it within two or three years of the vintage while the purple fruit is still vivid; Dolcetto is not a keeper.

budget_wines.name: Produttori del Barbaresco Langhe Nebbiolo DOC
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Produttori del Barbaresco cooperative shop in Barbaresco; broad international distribution through specialist importers.
budget_wines.description: Declassified young-vine Barbaresco fruit from the grower cooperative. A baby Barbaresco of rose, red cherry and fine tannin, it is the cheapest honest way into the Nebbiolo grape.
budget_wines.tip: It is the best-value Nebbiolo in the Langhe; buy a case to drink while your real Barbaresco rests in the cellar.

budget_wines.name: Pio Cesare Dolcetto d'Alba DOC
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Pio Cesare historic cellar in central Alba; distributed widely in Italy and the United States.
budget_wines.description: Classic Alba Dolcetto from the long-established Pio Cesare house. Deep violet, juicy black-cherry fruit and a clean, faintly bitter close, made for the everyday table.
budget_wines.tip: A house that built its name on Barolo also makes one of Alba's most reliable cheap Dolcetto; safe to buy unseen.

budget_wines.name: Coppo L'Avvocata Barbera d'Asti DOCG
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Coppo's underground Canelli cellars (a UNESCO site); distributed across Italy and exported widely.
budget_wines.description: Coppo's entry Barbera from Canelli, all stainless steel to keep the fruit forward. Bright red-berry juice, mouth-watering acidity and no oak: a true under-fifteen-euro find.
budget_wines.tip: From the Coppo house famous for its UNESCO underground cellars; L'Avvocata is the wine to pour by the carafe.

budget_wines.name: Malvira Roero Arneis DOCG
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Malvira cellar at Canale in the Roero; distributed in Italy and through US and UK importers.
budget_wines.description: Family-run Roero Arneis from Canale, the variety's heartland. White peach, almond and chamomile with a saline lift, it is Piedmont's best-value indigenous white.
budget_wines.tip: Arneis loses its delicacy with age; pick the youngest vintage and serve it cold with fritto misto or raw fish.

budget_wines.name: La Scolca Gavi dei Gavi White Label entry, Gavi DOCG
budget_wines.where_to_buy: La Scolca estate at Rovereto di Gavi; widely distributed in Italy, the US and Asia.
budget_wines.description: Cortese from the historic Gavi house at Rovereto. Crisp green-apple and citrus with a flinty, mineral finish, the dry white that put Gavi on the map at an everyday price for its black-label sibling's cellar.
budget_wines.tip: The basic Gavi from this estate over-delivers; reserve the famous black-label Gavi dei Gavi for a splurge.

budget_wines.name: Trediberri Langhe Nebbiolo DOC
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Trediberri cellar at Torriglione, La Morra; distributed in the US through Polaner Selections and others.
budget_wines.description: Organic young-vine Nebbiolo from La Morra fruit, made in a clean, fragrant style. Pale garnet, wild strawberry and rose, light tannin: an early-drinking window into La Morra terroir.
budget_wines.tip: Made from declassified La Morra Barolo fruit; one of the most elegant cheap Nebbiolos for the price.

budget_wines.name: G.D. Vajra Langhe Nebbiolo DOC
budget_wines.where_to_buy: G.D. Vajra cellar at Vergne, Barolo; distributed widely in Italy, the UK and the US.
budget_wines.description: Certified-organic Nebbiolo from younger Barolo-zone vines. Rose petal, sour cherry and tar in miniature, with friendly tannin, it is a budget rehearsal for the estate's Barolo Albe.
budget_wines.tip: Drink it alongside the estate's Barolo Albe to see how the same fruit scales up; the family farms both organically.

budget_wines.name: Marchesi di Barolo Ruvei Barbera d'Alba DOC
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Marchesi di Barolo cellar and enoteca in Barolo village; distributed across Italy and exported widely.
budget_wines.description: Barbera d'Alba from the historic Marchesi di Barolo house, partly aged in oak. Dark cherry, a touch of spice and a rounder, food-friendly body than the steel-only Asti versions.
budget_wines.tip: From the estate at the heart of dry Barolo's invention; Ruvei is its accessible everyday red with a little oak polish.
